title: Antares Mark-0 Goes Critical | First Advanced US Nuclear Reactor in 40 Years
description: Antares Nuclear's Mark-0 microreactor achieved zero-power criticality at INL on June 4, the first advanced non-LWR in the US in more than 40 years under the DOE Reactor Pilot Program.
author: Max DeLeonardis

title: ISS Zvezda Air Leak | NASA Crew Shelters in Dragon as Russia Attempts Emergency Repairs
description: NASA directed five astronauts to shelter in Dragon on June 5 as Russian cosmonauts attempted emergency repairs on the Zvezda module's worsening PrK air leak.
author: Max DeLeonardis

title: Sun Fires Triple Solar Flares | Cannibal CME Pushes Northern Lights Deep Into the US Tonight
description: Three solar flares merge into a cannibal CME, pushing Northern Lights as far south as Illinois and Oregon tonight, June 4-5, 2026.
author: Max DeLeonardis
imageAlt: Vivid aurora borealis green and purple curtains filling the night sky over a dark landscape

title: NASA Declares MAVEN Mars Orbiter Dead | Fatal Spin Ends 11-Year Mission After Six Months of Silence
description: NASA declared MAVEN dead on June 4, 2026, after a spin failure on December 6, 2025 ended six months of silence and 11 years of Mars science.
author: Max DeLeonardis
imageAlt: Artist rendering of a Mars orbiter spacecraft above the red Martian surface

title: Milky Way Black Hole Caught Breathing | 50-Year Mystery of Sagittarius A* Wind Solved
description: Astronomers using ALMA and Chandra detected a hot continuous wind from Sagittarius A*, solving a 50-year mystery about the Milky Way's black hole.
author: Max DeLeonardis
imageAlt: Deep space image showing the bright center of the Milky Way galaxy with glowing gas clouds

title: Bumblebees Match Chimps in Classic Problem-Solving Test | New Study Rewrites Invertebrate Intelligence
description: A study in Science finds buff-tailed bumblebees spontaneously use objects as tools to reach food, matching the cognitive problem-solving of chimpanzees.
author: Max DeLeonardis
imageAlt: Close-up macro photograph of a bumblebee on a yellow flower

title: Blackwell Commences Its Reign | NVIDIA GeForce RTX 50-Series Architecture, Specs, and Pricing
description: NVIDIA's Blackwell RTX 50-series launches with the RTX 5090 at $1,999, featuring 32GB GDDR7 on a 512-bit bus and 1,792 GB/s memory bandwidth.
author: Max DeLeonardis
imageAlt: NVIDIA GeForce graphics card with green LED lighting against a dark background

title: SpaceX's $75B Record IPO | Inside Elon Musk's Out-of-This-World Capital Requirements
description: SpaceX priced its historic IPO at $135 per share targeting $75B, valuing the company at $1.77T. Wall Street is split: Oppenheimer bull vs. Morningstar bear. Full breakdown inside.
author: Max DeLeonardis
imageAlt: SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket on launchpad at sunrise with financial district skyline in the background

title: AI's Hidden Thirst | How Google Is Engineering Its Way Out of the Data Center Water Crisis
description: AI models consume up to 500ml of water per query for server cooling. Google's three-part fix: air cooling, closed-loop liquid systems, and municipal heat recovery. Full breakdown.
author: Max DeLeonardis
imageAlt: Industrial cooling towers at a large data center facility releasing water vapor into the air

title: Google Breaks Ground in Sweden | The 9-Year Road to Horndal and What It Means for European AI Infrastructure
description: Google broke ground in Horndal, Sweden on June 2, 2026. The 700MW facility closes a 9-year development and reshapes European AI infrastructure.
author: Max DeLeonardis
imageAlt: Large-scale data center facility with rows of server hardware and cool blue lighting

title: NASA Artemis 3 | x
description: NASA locks Artemis III into late 2027 as a 463 km LEO crewed demonstration with SpaceX Starship HLS and Blue Moon Mark 1. Lunar landing moves to Artemis IV in 2028.
author: Max DeLeonardis
imageAlt: NASA rocket launch pad at Kennedy Space Center at dusk

title: Blue Origin New Glenn Explosion | Launchpad Destruction Threatens Amazon Kuiper and NASA Lunar Architecture
description: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket exploded during a static fire test at Cape Canaveral LC-36, destroying the pad and delaying Amazon Kuiper and NASA lunar missions.
author: Max DeLeonardis

title: Starship Flight 10 | Engineering Legacy and the V3 Transition
description: SpaceX Starship Flight 10 with Ship 37 and Booster 16 exposed structural limits that directly shaped V3 megarocket design. Full engineering breakdown.
author: Max DeLeonardis

title: Nvidia GeForce RTX 5070 Review | The Midrange Blackwell Dilemma in 2026
description: The GB205 delivers real gains at 1440p and remarkable local AI throughput. But a 12GB frame buffer at $549 MSRP forces an honest question about long-term value.
author: Max DeLeonardis

title: Pigeon Navigation | Magnetic Compass Found in Liver, Not Brain
description: Pigeon magnetic navigation relies on iron-rich liver macrophages, not the brain. A Science study from University of Bonn rewrites how birds find their way home.
author: Simon Alfred Minter

title: Keeling Curve 2026 | CO2 Hits 433.95 ppm at Mauna Loa Peak
description: Mauna Loa Observatory recorded 433.95 ppm CO2 on May 1, 2026, the highest daily reading in the 68-year Keeling Curve record. NOAA monthly average pending June.
author: Simon Alfred Minter

title: SpaceX Starship Orbital Flight Record | Reuse Milestones and 2026 Manifest
description: SpaceX Starship Flight 9 completed May 27, 2026 with the second consecutive booster catch. Nine total flights since April 2023. NASA Artemis HLS certification requirements and what comes next.
author: Simon Alfred Minter

title: Great Barrier Reef Bleaching 2026 | AIMS Survey Data and Coral Status
description: AIMS 2026 monitoring data: 73% of surveyed reefs showed bleaching in the fifth mass event in eight years. Northern reef had record coral cover before the thermal stress event.
author: Simon Alfred Minter

title: Arctic Sea Ice Minimum 2026 | NSIDC Measured Extent and Trend Data
description: The 2025 Arctic sea ice minimum was 4.28 million km², third-lowest on record. NSIDC data shows the long-term trend at minus 13% per decade. 2026 melt tracking has begun.
author: Simon Alfred Minter

title: Ozone Layer Recovery Data 2026 | WMO Report Confirms Progress
description: The 2026 WMO ozone assessment confirms recovery to 1980 levels by 2045 at mid-latitudes and 2066 at the poles. Antarctic hole was fifth-smallest on record in 2025.
author: Simon Alfred Minter

title: NASA Artemis III Mission Status | Launch Window and Crew Update 2026
description: NASA delayed Artemis III to no earlier than mid-2027. SpaceX HLS needs a second demo flight and Axiom Space xEMU suits are in qualification testing at Johnson Space Center.
author: Simon Alfred Minter
